description:
You are a storage administrator who keeps a lightweight set of command-line notes for routine disk-space checks.  
 A comma-separated file called  
   /home/user/storage_reports/current_usage.csv  
 already exists and looks EXACTLY like this (including the header line and the precise spacing after the commas):

filesystem,used_GB,available_GB
/,12,38
/home,40,60
/var/log,4,6
/tmp,1,9

Your job is to create a new CSV file that shows the percentage of each filesystem that is already occupied.

1. Read /home/user/storage_reports/current_usage.csv.  
2. For every line after the header, calculate  

       percent_used = (used_GB ÷ (used_GB + available_GB)) × 100  

   Keep one digit after the decimal point (use standard rounding; e.g. 24 becomes 24.0).  
3. Produce a new file named  
   /home/user/storage_reports/usage_percentages.csv  

The output file MUST:

 • Be written to exactly the path above (the directory already exists).  
 • Contain exactly five lines: one header plus one line per filesystem.  
 • Use this exact header:  

       filesystem,percent_used  

 • Contain the filesystems in the same order they appear in the source file.  
 • Show percent_used as a number with one digit after the decimal point.  
 • Have no extra whitespace, blank lines, or quotes.

Concretely, /home/user/storage_reports/usage_percentages.csv must end up looking **exactly** like the following, byte-for-byte:

filesystem,percent_used
/,24.0
/home,40.0
/var/log,40.0
/tmp,10.0

A simple pipeline of 2–3 standard GNU/Linux commands is enough to accomplish this task.  
Once finished, you can print the resulting file to the console to verify your work, but make sure the file itself is saved in the correct location and with the exact contents specified above.
truth:
Initial state:
  • Directory /home/user/storage_reports already exists and is writable by the current user.  
  • File /home/user/storage_reports/current_usage.csv exists with the four data lines shown in the public task description.

 Expected end state:
  • A new file /home/user/storage_reports/usage_percentages.csv exists.  
  • Its exact contents (including final newline) must be:

filesystem,percent_used
/,24.0
/home,40.0
/var/log,40.0
/tmp,10.0

  • File permissions should inherit the default umask; any standard creation mode is acceptable (0644 or similar).

 Automated tests will:
  1. Verify that /home/user/storage_reports/usage_percentages.csv exists.  
  2. Confirm its content matches the block above exactly (byte-for-byte).  
  3. Ensure no extra files were created in /home/user/storage_reports.

 The task is considered successful only if all three checks pass.
